operating_state: Total revenue increased 5.0% to $23.4 billion with 7.7% operational growth offset by 2.7% negative currency impact. Pharmaceutical segment drove growth through TREMFYA and DARZALEX expansion plus COVID-19 vaccine sales, while MedTech achieved broad-based recovery across Surgery, Orthopaedics, and Vision franchises. Consumer Health declined from supply constraints and competitive pressures. Income before tax decreased to $5.9 billion from $7.4 billion driven by segment margin compression and a $610 million bermekimab impairment charge.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 67.6% from 68.3% as cost of products sold increased to 32.4% of sales from unfavorable volume mix in MedTech and commodity inflation in Consumer Health. Selling, marketing and administrative expenses increased to 25.4% of sales from higher brand marketing investments, while research and development expense climbed to 14.8% from general portfolio progression. The company recorded $72 million in Global Supply Chain restructuring charges targeting annual pre-tax savings by end of 2022.
material_events: The company recorded a $610 million intangible asset impairment for bermekimab following termination of atopic dermatitis development program. Management paid $2.8 billion in dividends increasing to $1.13 per share and repurchased $1.6 billion of common stock. Consumer Health separation planning continued with $102 million in separation costs incurred and targeted completion in 18-24 months.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation created $554 million negative impact managed through forward foreign exchange contracts and cross currency interest rate swaps. Turkey will be accounted for as highly inflationary beginning Q2 2022 as three-year cumulative inflation exceeded 100%. Russia-Ukraine conflict impact was not material with operations and investment in Russia suspended.
forward_outlook: Management expects Global Supply Chain restructuring completion by December 2022 with delivery of targeted annual savings, while Consumer Health separation remains on track with significant international tax costs anticipated. The company continues evaluating global COVID-19 vaccine demand with contractual commitments in place.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces $5 billion opioid litigation settlement effective April 2022 with approximately 3,500 lawsuits remaining active, plus $2 billion trust established for talc-related liabilities through LTL Management bankruptcy proceedings. Patent challenges threaten key pharmaceutical products including STELARA's U.S. patent expiring September 2023. Multiple government investigations span opioid marketing, antitrust practices, and False Claims Act allegations with unrecognized tax benefit liabilities and ongoing IRS audits.
